title: Pseudopteryxia hendersonii
intro: Pseudopteryxia hendersonii, commonly known as Henderson's Pseudopteryxia, is a plant species endemic to Western Australia, belonging to the family Apiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The bioactive constituents of Pseudopteryxia hendersonii include sesquiterpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Pseudopteryxia hendersonii is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or dried powder, and is used to treat various health conditions, including skin infections, wounds, and respiratory issues.

The plant's classification is further divided into the genus Pseudopteryxia and the species P. hendersonii.
